documentType: MCP server configuration and tool catalog

paper.title: Knowledge Graph-Grounded Functional Requirements Generation for Domain-Specific Languages via Repository Mining
paper.description: The MCP server exposes a repository-mined Neo4j knowledge graph through three structured query tools. The tools retrieve frequent concepts, dominant concept relationships, and multi-level concept neighborhoods for grounding DSL functional-requirements generation.

serverMetadata.name: neo4j-graph-server
serverMetadata.toolDiscovery: Tool definitions are exposed at runtime through the MCP tools/list operation.

tools.name: neo4j_top_concepts
tools.description: Return the most frequently occurring concepts in the Knowledge Graph, ordered by occurrence count in descending order.
tools.paperPurpose: Surfaces the concepts that appear most persistently across the analyzed repositories. It is most appropriate when the target DSL addresses a dominant domain in the Knowledge Graph.

limit.description: Maximum number of concept nodes to return. The server clamps the value to the range 1 through 2000.

result.description: An object containing the requested limit and a nodes array ordered by count in descending order.

fields.nodes: Array of objects with concept and count fields.

tools.name: neo4j_top_related_concepts
tools.description: Return the most dominant RELATED_TO concept pairs in the Knowledge Graph, ordered by final relationship weight in descending order.
tools.paperPurpose: Surfaces the concept associations that appear most persistently across the analyzed repositories. It is most appropriate when the target DSL addresses a dominant domain in the Knowledge Graph.

limit.description: Maximum number of concept relationships to return. The server clamps the value to the range 1 through 2000.

result.description: An object containing the requested limit and an edges array ordered by relationship weight in descending order.

fields.edges: Array of objects with source, target, weight, and count fields.

tools.name: neo4j_neighbors_for_domain
tools.description: Return a multi-level neighborhood for a given concept in the Neo4j Knowledge Graph. The search proceeds level by level, using the neighbors found at one level as the starting concepts for the next level.
tools.paperPurpose: Retrieves grounded knowledge for any domain represented in the Knowledge Graph. For compound domain names, the tool can be invoked separately for the abbreviation and each constituent concept.

domain_name.description: Required seed concept name from which the neighborhood search begins. Concept names used by the requirements-generation prompt are lowercase.

max_level.description: Number of neighborhood levels to explore. Level 1 returns direct neighbors; larger values recursively explore the concepts found in the preceding level.

per_level_limit.description: Maximum number of direct neighbors retrieved for each seed concept at each level.

global_limit.description: Maximum total number of concept-neighbor relationships returned across all levels.

result.description: An object containing the effective search arguments and a neighbors array. Results are sorted by level ascending and, within each level, by final relationship weight descending.

fields.domain_name: The root concept supplied by the caller.
fields.neighbors: Array of objects with domain_name, neighbor, level, and final_weight fields.
